etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "tree; wood") + phonetic 冊(冊) (cè, "a form made by lining up pieces of wood and binding them together") -> fence
etymology.interpretation: The character 柵 is composed of 木(木) (mù, "tree; wood"), suggesting materials made of wood, combined with the phonetic 冊(冊) (cè, "a form made by lining up pieces of wood and binding them together"), which conveys the sense of arranging wood in a linear order. This fittingly describes a structure like a fence or palisade, which is essentially wood sections lined together.

parts.english: a form made by lining up pieces of wood and binding them together

examples.chinese: 农场周围有一道高高的栅栏(農場周圍有一道高高的柵欄)。
examples.pinyin: Nóngchǎng zhōuwéi yǒu yīdào gāogāo de zhàlán.
examples.english: There is a high fence surrounding the farm.

examples.chinese: 小路的尽头是一扇古老的木栅门(小路的盡頭是一扇古老的木柵門)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎolù de jìntóu shì yī shàn gǔlǎo de mù zhàmén.
examples.english: At the end of the path lies an old wooden gate.

examples.chinese: 工人正在修理受损的栅栏(工人正在修理受損的柵欄)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngrén zhèngzài xiūlǐ shòusǔn de zhàlán.
examples.english: The worker is repairing the damaged fence.
